title: [PyCDE] Auto-name signals from Python variable names
body:
Add bytecode inspection to automatically derive signal names from the Python variable they are assigned to. For example, `x = comb.AddOp(a, b)` will auto-name the resulting signal "x" without requiring a manual `.name = "x"` call.

The tracer inspects the calling frame's bytecode after a CALL instruction to detect STORE_FAST/STORE_NAME/STORE_DEREF (simple variable assignment) and STORE_SUBSCR (subscript like `d[0]`, `d["key"]`, `d[var]`). Auto-naming is skipped for bare `_` assignments, attribute stores, tuple unpacking, and pycde-internal code (except for an allowlist of modules like pycde.constructs and pycde.bsp.*).

A new `debug` parameter on `System()` switches auto-naming from `sv.namehint` attributes to `hw.wire` ops with inner symbols, creating optimization barriers that guarantee named wires appear in the output SystemVerilog.

New files:
- pycde/tracer.py: bytecode inspection utility (get_var_name)
- test/test_tracer.py: unit tests for the tracer
- test/test_debug_mode.py: integration test for debug mode hw.wire insertion, verifying wires survive canonicalization and appear in SV

AI-assisted-by: GitHub Copilot (Claude)
